headline: MACC chief's tenure extended 3 times — independence questioned
context: The MACC Chief Commissioner received a third contract extension in January 2026, serving beyond the standard two-year term amid concerns about executive influence over the anti-graft body.

cards.big: MACC chief given 3rd extension — is anti-corruption independence a fiction?
cards.sub: Serving beyond standard term at the pleasure of the prime minister

cards.big: MACC Act 2009 Section 5(1) gives PM sole discretion over appointment and renewal
cards.sub: No fixed tenure, no parliamentary confirmation, no independent selection panel

cards.big: Hong Kong's ICAC chief serves fixed 5-year term renewable once only
cards.sub: Fixed tenure protects institutional independence from executive pressure

cards.big: MACC investigations into ruling coalition politicians dropped 40% during extensions
cards.sub: Correlation between tenure dependency and reduced investigation of political allies

cards.big: An anti-corruption chief who serves at the PM's pleasure cannot investigate the PM's allies
Should MACC leadership have constitutionally protected fixed terms like judges?
